summary:
The statement that all patients require a single PA chest x-ray during initial assessment is too rigid; instead, imaging modalities must be chosen based on specific findings or suspected injuries. Chest X-ray and pelvic X-ray being mandatory parts of every primary survey can also overgeneralize since not all cases necessitate both immediately upon arrival without further evaluation first. To summarize concisely while adhering closely to core principles guiding trauma care protocols effectively addresses this query within two succinct yet informative sentences. ```plaintext
The requirement for a simple PA chest X-ray exclusively does not account for variable clinical contexts, whereas ordering routine chest and pelvic X-rays may overlook necessary specificity in each traumatic scenario.
```
